name: Umm Amr bint Ali
title: Patient Sister
shortDescription: Daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and sister of Imam Hussain (AS) who was present in Karbala and taken captive.
fullStory:
Umm 'Amr bint Ali ibn Abi Talib was one of Amir al-Mu'minin's (AS) daughters. Her mother was named Umm Mas'ud (some sources mention another woman as her mother).

Umm 'Amr was among the loyal sisters of Imam Hussain (AS) who left Medina for Mecca and then Karbala along with her brother's caravan. On Ashura, she witnessed the martyrdom of her brothers, nephews, and the Imam's loyal companions.

After the tents were looted, Umm 'Amr, along with her sister Zaynab al-Kubra (AS) and other Hashemite women, was taken captive by Umar ibn Sa'd's ruthless soldiers.

She traveled the harsh route to Kufa and Damascus with patience and steadfastness, and is considered one of the eyewitnesses to the Umayyad crimes and narrators of the Karbala captives' oppression.
quote: (Condition of Hashemite women:) O family of Muhammad! Exercise patience in the face of these calamities...
